title: Today's AI Brief: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
summary: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business goes global, and a Māori Text-to-Speech Model challenges big tech values.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
imageCaption: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
imageSourceTitle: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. Let's dive into the latest news. First up, Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally. For years, WhatsApp has been a go-to communication layer for businesses worldwide. By infusing AI into this layer, Meta aims to turn WhatsApp into workflow software for small and medium-sized businesses. This move makes sense, given WhatsApp's massive user base - over 2 billion users, after all! The AI agent can help businesses automate tasks, provide customer support, and even help with sales. It's an interesting play by Meta to expand WhatsApp's capabilities beyond personal messaging.

In other news, a Māori Text-to-Speech Model is making waves by challenging big tech's values. This model, developed for the indigenous Māori language of New Zealand, prioritizes cultural sensitivity and community involvement. It's a great example of AI being developed with a specific cultural context in mind, rather than a one-size-fits-all approach. And it's not just about the tech itself, but also about who gets to decide how it's developed and used. Kudos to the team for pushing the boundaries of what AI can do in a responsible way.

Māori Text-to-Speech Model Spurns Big Tech’s Values is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: New Zealand is a country famed for its dramatic landscapes, but its linguistic landscape is arguably just as interesting. Of its three official languages, only te reo Māori (the Māori language) could be described as indigenous. Though spoken fluently by just 4.3 percent of the population, national statistics show that about 30 percent of New Zealanders can ... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: This week on Uncanny Valley , we discuss why the race among the top AI companies to go public has just gotten started but is already creating bizarre repercussions—including numerous San Francisco real estate listings preferring Anthropic’s stock instead of cash. They also get into why Donald Trump’s new executive order on AI safety is underwhelming, and ho... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
